scope: Pass B full-surface expansion against the live OpenAPI spec published at https://developers.squarespace.com/commerce-apis/latest/schema-processor-version-version-latest.json (fetched and enumerated directly): 8 read streams (the 4 wave2 streams retained at parity — note the bundle's own request path for products, GET /commerce/products against a base_url that already includes /1.0, does not match the real API's /v2/commerce/products path; this pre-existing wave2 stream is left untouched per the meta-rule against altering already-migrated behavior, and the mismatch is called out in Known limits — plus transactions, store_pages, webhook_subscriptions, contacts) and 4 write actions (contact create/delete, webhook subscription create/delete). Excluded: product/inventory/order mutation endpoints whose real request bodies are non-flat (Squarespace's own partial-update convention wraps every changed field as {"present":true,"value":...}, or wraps operations as named arrays of {variantId,quantity} objects) and therefore cannot be constructed by the engine's default JSON write body (record fields minus path_fields, each copied verbatim) without a Tier-2 WriteHook — out of scope for a declarative-only Pass B pass; asset/image binary upload endpoints; org-tier authorization/member introspection; and single-object detail endpoints that duplicate an already-covered list stream's record shape.

excluded.reason: Order.create's real body is a full order graph (lineItems, addresses, fulfillmentStatus, discountLines) with no flat-field shape suitable for reverse ETL; creating orders programmatically is also a rare/unsafe integration pattern for a data-sync connector
single-order detail; identical record shape to the orders list stream's per-item object, already covered by that stream

OrderFulfillmentRequest's body is a shipments[] array of nested CreateOrderShipmentRequest objects (carrierName/service/shipDate/trackingNumber each), not a flat record; the engine's default JSON write body copies top-level record fields verbatim and cannot construct a nested array-of-objects payload without a Tier-2 WriteHook
single/batch transaction detail by id; identical record shape to the transactions list stream's per-item object, already covered by that stream
batch inventory-item detail by variant id; identical record shape to the inventory list stream's per-item object, already covered by that stream

CreateInventoryAdjustmentRequest's body is 4 alternative named arrays (incrementOperations/decrementOperations/setFiniteOperations/setUnlimitedOperations) of {variantId,quantity} objects, not a flat record; the engine's default JSON write body cannot construct this nested array-of-operations shape without a Tier-2 WriteHook
batch profile detail by id; identical record shape to the profiles list stream's per-item object, already covered by that stream
single webhook-subscription detail; identical record shape to the webhook_subscriptions list stream's per-item object, already covered by that stream

ExternalUpdateWebhookSubscriptionRequest wraps every changed field as {present:true,value:...} (Squarespace's own partial-update convention), not a flat record; the engine's default JSON write body copies top-level record fields verbatim and cannot construct this per-field wrapper without a Tier-2 WriteHook
immediately invalidates the subscription's existing signing secret for every downstream consumer verifying webhook signatures; an operator-initiated security action, not a syncable data mutation
fires a synthetic test event at the configured endpoint for manual verification; no data is created, read, or mutated
returns metadata about the Squarespace member who owns the OAuth token in use, not syncable site data
returns metadata about the website that owns the API key/OAuth token in use, not syncable site data

the Contacts API v1 surface lives at a different version prefix (/v1/...) than every Commerce API write action's base_url (which bakes in the Commerce API's /1.0 segment); reaching it requires the same stream.path absolute-URL override the contacts READ stream uses, but write actions have no equivalent conformance skip-marker mechanism (write_request_shape's replay capture server always points at b.HTTP.URL, which an absolute-URL action.path bypasses entirely) — so an untestable write action was not added rather than shipping one that cannot be proven correct in this repo's conformance harness

filtered/sorted contact search returning the identical Contact record shape already covered by the contacts list stream; a POST-with-body search endpoint has no GET-list declarative equivalent in this dialect
single-contact detail; identical record shape to the contacts list stream's per-item object, already covered by that stream
requires an application/merge-patch+json content type the engine's write dialect (json/form/none body_type) does not model
same Contacts API v1 version-prefix/conformance-testability gap as POST /v1/contacts above; not added as an untestable write action
sub-resource of an individual contact (address book entries); no parent-id fan-out stream is declared for contacts in this pass, so there is no id source to drive this per-contact sub-read
creates an address-book entry under a specific contact; narrow sub-resource write with no corresponding read stream in this pass
single address-book-entry detail; no parent address-book stream is covered in this pass
replaces a specific address-book entry; narrow sub-resource write with no corresponding read stream in this pass
deletes a specific address-book entry; narrow sub-resource write with no corresponding read stream in this pass
returns aggregated order/donation totals per contact for a caller-supplied contact-id batch, not a syncable object list

CreateProductRequest's body includes nested variants[]/variantAttributes[] arrays required for most real product types; not expressible as the engine's default flat JSON write body without a Tier-2 WriteHook
batch product detail by id; identical record shape to the products list stream's per-item object, already covered by that stream

UpdateProductRequest wraps every changed field as {present:true,value:...} (Squarespace's own partial-update convention), not a flat record; the engine's default JSON write body copies top-level record fields verbatim and cannot construct this per-field wrapper without a Tier-2 WriteHook
permanently deletes a product and all its variants/images from the merchant's live storefront; higher-risk storefront-content deletion, out of scope for this pass's reverse-ETL write set
multipart image upload; the engine's write dialect has no multipart/binary body construction
updates image metadata (altText) only; narrow sub-resource write with no corresponding read stream
deletes a single product image; narrow sub-resource write with no corresponding read stream
reorders product images; narrow sub-resource write with no corresponding read stream
returns async image-processing status only, not syncable product data
CreateProductVariantRequest's body includes nested attributes/pricing/shippingMeasurements objects; narrow sub-resource write with no corresponding read stream
UpdateProductVariantRequest uses the same present/value wrapper convention as update_product; narrow sub-resource write with no corresponding read stream
deletes a single product variant; narrow sub-resource write with no corresponding read stream
associates an existing image with a variant; narrow sub-resource write with no corresponding read stream
